title: Serjania angusta
intro: Serjania angusta is a plant species belonging to the Sapindaceae family, commonly known as the soapberry family, and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The leaves and fruits of Serjania angusta have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to their purported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Serjania angusta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Serjania angusta is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its most common preparations include tea and tincture, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and flu.
